metadata.title:
NATURAL DRAIN


IS CH0KEO

RESIDENT SHOWS WHERE
BLUNDER IS RESPONSIBLE
FOR FLOODS IN UNION PARK
DISTRICT AND TELLS REM
EDY.

pages.raw_text:
According to J. B. Mullins there
is no nee dof floods in the Union
Park district, even without a sew
err, if the city will remove obstruc-
tons it has placed in the natural
outlet of surplus water He says
there is dry slough running from
the southeast part of the city to-
wards East Olive st. bridge which
will serve under any ordinary con-
ditions, but this has been filled in
several places. When the city took
up defective cement sidewalk on
Third and Fourth avs. the cement
was dumped into the slough at
Stone and Lee SITS.


AS Mullins has figured it out, II
these distractions were removed
the outlet is the cheapest and
surest way to drain into the river.
He holds that no sewer the city
can build would take care of more
than small portion of the water
flowing in when heavy fall of
snow is melted in a few hours.
